title: Terminal reset on logs complete
body:
Signed-off-by: Parker Van Roy <pvanroy@redhat.com>

Response for https://github.com/containers/podman/issues/7250#event-3742016507
Although, I did this without a reproducer and assumed it would work. @tanwald @mheon if you know of any I will test it out

Detects if the os.Stdout is a terminal (or cygwin terminal) and resets colors on complete if so. (also adds an extra newline)

comments.body:
[APPROVALNOTIFIER] This PR is **APPROVED** This pull-request has been approved by: *mheon*, *ParkerVR* The full list of commands accepted by this bot can be found [here](https://go.k8s.io/bot-commands?repo=containers%2Fpodman). The pull request process is described [here](https://git.k8s.io/community/contributors/guide/owners.md#the-code-review-process)
Needs approval from an approver in each of these files: - ~~[OWNERS](https://github.com/containers/podman/blob/master/OWNERS)~~ [mheon] Approvers can indicate their approval by writing `/approve` in a comment Approvers can cancel approval by writing `/approve cancel` in a comment
Vendor check is angry - you might want to swap to `terminal.IsTerminal()` which is already vendored?

I understand why someone want this fixed by podman but I don't think it would make much sense.
1. Someone might have set their color code to red for example and they wouldn't want it to be reset by podman.
2. Other linux utilities like `cat` and `tail` don't do this either. Just try:
```
$ echo -e '\033[0;31mtest' > tmp
$ cat tmp
```
3. I think it is very unlikely to end up in this state. If a program writes color codes to the terminal they would normally reset them.
4. You can reset this yourself automatically for all programs if you put the reset code into your `$PS1`.
I agree, lets close this as won't fix.  I don't like resetting the terminal on completion, since this might reset from a known setting.
